title: Why did Shinji reject instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Why did Shinji reject instrumentality ?             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Wed Oct 30, 2013 6:24 am Hello everybody , I was watching EoE for the third time now and I still don't have a single clue what exactly made Shinji thinking, that the psychological issues he suffers from are okay as long he can see the people again. The psychological dream scenes in the Pre 3I Instrumentality are seemingly having four parts: - the sandbox scene - the sex scene - the train scene - the strangling scene It's very hard for me to get a roughly idea what psychological process every single scene represents. So my questions are: - What exactly did drove Shinji into rejecting the instrumentality? - What does every single scene mean to Shinji's psychological development (with the final outcome of rejecting the instrumentality) ? P.S. I'm not a native speaker, don't be mean ^^

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 30, 2013 1:47 pm Uh... wow. That's a hell of a first post. Sorry to say that's a VERY big question and we don't have a single definitive answer to give you. Each of those parts has been discussed in-depth multiple times with varying interpretations. You pretty much need to just do some lurking around older threads and make up your own mind, or ask more specific questions. Also I'm not sure how the two parts of your question really go together. The scenes in pre-instrumentality are why he triggered instrumentality, not why he rejected it. That has to be based on his equally complex experiences after instrumentality has already begun.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Wed Oct 30, 2013 3:15 pm Okay, I guess I messed my question up a little First of all: I couldn't find any thread covering that topic, which is why I started this thread in the firt place. I would be very thankful if you couldgive me a link to one of the threads you were talking about . Now I’m trying to explain what I meant: 1. What drove Shinji into approve the 3. Impact / instrumentality? This is the question I can mostly explain myself (although my explanation seems a little bit odd to myself): In the 4 Sequences Shinji comes to the conclusion, that he don't need anybody or rather hates everybody but at the same time he wants to be accepted: In the Sandbox-scene, he gets (from his point of view) betrayed in a social way (furthermore there’s a butt load of other stuff to interpret in this scene) In the train scenes Rei and Misato are offering him help and approval, but he fears getting betrayed; In the sex-scene they try to show him, that being social (I know this formulation seems pretty dumb but don't know how to describe it better) isn't painful, but he still fears it In the last scene, Asuka, one of the rare persons Shinji seems to like, rejects him because of his anxiety and his inclination of escaping. This causes him to succumb his social fears (they seem a lot like a schizoid personality disorder ... has anybody utter this thought before ? ) and he develops a kind of Misanthropy in combination with the morbid wish of approval (which is "(cock) blocked" by his paranoid fear of getting betrayed) which gives him a motive for escaping into the instrumentality along with "killing" everyone he hates that much (or at least erasing them in the kind he knows them). 2. What drove Shinji into rejecting instrumentality? This is the part where I have completely no clue what is going on in Shinji's mind. He's talking with Rei about dreams, reality and in some way how he is deluded in his perception of reality. This causes him to decide that he won't escape into instrumentality, shows him that pain is an important part of life and that he can only be lucky if he is hurt? Yeah I don't really get the point in this climax, so this is my main question. As I said before, I would be really happy if anyone could give me a roughly approach to the topic of this two question or link me to a suitable thread. Thank you in anticipation.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 30, 2013 5:17 pm Some links worth checking out (some of them quite old): [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3806/Pre-3I-Kitchen-Scene-Memory-or-Mind-Meld/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6462/P3II-Questions-Asuka-or-Not/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13121/The-Sandbox-Sequence-and-the-Fourth-Wall/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/5055/Do-You-Want-to-Become-One-With-Me/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/12382/Why-was-Shinji-strangling-Asuka-at-the-end-of-EoE/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/post/229527/EoE-Instrumentality-101/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14267/Post-Death-Kaji-included-in-EoEs-Instrumentality/[/url] I'll take a stab at it briefly: Gorbatschow wrote: 1. What drove Shinji into approve the 3. Impact / instrumentality?

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Wed Oct 30, 2013 5:33 pm 1. What drove Shinji into approve the 3. Impact / instrumentality? Trauma, after trauma, after trauma until he broke and decided "fuck it and fuck you, I'm going to check other neighbourhood" 2. What drove Shinji into rejecting instrumentality? On the other side, Shinji experienced vision in which Anno was hammering his philosophy into him in a form of strange noises, subconscious commands, flashing colors and hallucionations. Shinji couldn't stand this bullshit anymore so he escaped back to earth.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Oct 30, 2013 5:50 pm Gorbatschow, welcome! I'm glad you're getting into this as fast as possible. I would advise taking NemZ's advice, and delving deeply into the links that he's given you. There's a lot of great analysis floating around here for you to take advantage of.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Oct 30, 2013 7:34 pm Gorbatschow wrote: 1. What drove Shinji into approve the 3. Impact / instrumentality? 2. What drove Shinji into rejecting instrumentality?

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Mon Nov 04, 2013 10:00 am Sorry for the late answer I want to thank everyone who answered to my questions (especially NemZ). I already read a quite big part of the linked threads and I’m realizing, that I really need to rewatch the show Thanks to everyone

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 04, 2013 7:25 pm 1. First, consider that this takes place the next day after Kaworu's death. And he's the last one in a long, long series of people Shinji cared about that either die, get separated from him, or otherwise get screwed over. After a couple of months like these, everyone would be in a mindset of "I just don't care anymore". Then, as a double whammy, Misato and Asuka gets butchered before his eyes, and he gets a lovecraftian ritual performed on him - The dispair and frustration that came to light here had been present for a long time, but it's the circumstances that forced them out to the daylight. Basically, by the time GNK appeared before him and tanged him, he was in so much pain that he'd be willing to accept anything that would make it stop. And that's exactly what the whole ritual is supposed to create: A world without suffering. The pre-instrumentality/instrumentality mindfuck scenes were partially a representation of everyone's minds melting into each other (EVA 01 becomes the "source of all souls" - So, he meets all the dead/previously collected people. Misato. Asuka. We even briefly hear Ritsuko.) The kitchen scene is basically a discharge of all the pent-up frustrated feeling between Shinji and Asuka, they're saying out loud what they were always thinking behind their masks (Note that they're wearing the same clothes as in the kiss scene), but she's symbolic When he snaps her neck, he finally snaps from all her abuse and releases all that pent-up anger, but he also generally rejects the existence with "others" - which is why TI follows immediately afterwards, and the people we know become less distinquishable as humanity becomes a huge clump of thoughts. Note that at some point, Shinji becomes aware that not everybody liked this ("But you told me nothing would be unpleasant here! You betrayed me! [like Kaworu and Gendo]" "No. you misunderstood what the deal was. You thought everybody would feel the same way as you") This is also manifested by these random voices of §female rejection" going with the instrumentality = Sex theme. The last thing we hear is "Coward!" - Asuka resisted merging with Shinji for the longest. The sandbox scene is a general symbolic representation of his time at NERV, a mix of memories and dream symbolism. The pyramid may be his relationship with Gendo. He was all alone and had no mommy to go home with, so he worked very hard at his work to build the pyramid. But then, frustrated that he weas still alone he kicked it all down in a tantrum (HIs final fallout with Gendo after Bardiel) - and then, miserably tried fixing it somehow... It cozuld also be that subjective feeling of his that he breaks everything he touches. According to the script, the random "Mommy" is supposed to look like Misato, and the creepy children are meant to look like Asuka and Rei - He's filling in unclear memories with stuff from the present. The RL sequences are Shinji and Rei contemplating the final result. ("Do you feel good", aka, is this what you weanted?) Maybe the RL is supposed to represent seeing things through the eyes/memories of other tanged people. Throughout all of this, the cross is representative of his promise to Misato to protect the world - this is why he willingly lets it go after he gets all comfy in the sea of LCL, but it won't quite disapear from his field of vision... and why he has it in his hand again after deciding to go back. 2. Three main reasons: a) The first, he says is pretty openly: He gave instrumentality a proper try, and it sucked. Sure, it really was a world without suffering through interactions with others, but it wasn't as if he was peacufully getting along with them in some magic paradise - without the boundaries of others to define us, so goes this show's philosophy, we can't even tell we exist. So it was more like becoming completely self-sufficient, or not existing at all - there's no sadness, but there's no happiness either, so it's totally not worth it. b)"I want to see them again, because my feelings were real." Straightforward enough; He recalls all the good times he had with the people in his life before everything went to crap, and how he really cared about them. So much, in fact, that he ends up deciding that being with them is worth all the scary painfull stuff he mentioned earlier. So he's basically finally accepting them all with all their good and bad sides. c)"love" and "hope". While every remotely positive experience he had with his surroundings entered into his final decision, two in particular stand up, the two people whose true devotion to him as a person, not just as a glorified remote control for EVA 01, was so obvious even Shinji's pessimism goggles couldn't deny them away. Rei and Kaworu - the ones who made this decisioon possible, and who probably can't follow him into the new world. Note the following points here: I Rei was also trying to talk him out of instrumentality for a good part of the process, allthough she was ready to go along with what he wants ("Then what is your hand for?" ) Of course, this quote is accompained by Kaworu in the background as a positive example of things you can do with hands, as if to balance the pissed Asuka before that. II This is the ultimate revelation of what exactly the apparitions of Rei in Shinji's who mind trip scenes was. (For example: Shinji: "Daddy hates me!" Mind!Rei: "Maybe you just don't understand him.") III Note how this is the exact opposite of Gendo's logic: Gendo: "In this cruel, dark world, I found one person who loved, accepted and understood me. Then I lost her. Therefore, the world is a cruel, hopeless place where you will lose everything. So i will disregard all Yui cared for and wreck it. " Finale!Shinji: "In this cruel, dark world, I found two person who loved, accepted and understood me. Then I lost them. Therefore, this world is a place where love and understanding can theoretically exist, and there's still hope for it. So I will live on with the lessons they taught me." Shinji was quite close to going down the very same world-hating path, but if he wasn't tempted at all, what sort of informed decision can that be?

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Thu Dec 05, 2013 2:04 am Sorry for bumping this old thread, but I guess it's better than creating a new one at the risk of overlooking an already existing thread with the same topic: After rewatching End of Evangelion two times with the informations of this and the linked threads, there's still one dialogue I don't get: Shinji "Hey" Rei "What is it?" Shinji "What are dreams?" Rei "Dreams?....Yes, dreams" Shinji "I don't understand... I don't understand what reality is" Rei "You can't bridge the gap between your own truth and the reality of others" Shinji "I don't know where to find happiness" Rei "So you only find happiness in your dreams" Shinji "Then..this is not reality?...This world where no one exists?" Rei "No it is only a dream" Shinji "Then I don't exist here ither" Rei "This convenient fabrication is your attempt to change reality" Shinji "Is that wrong?" Rei "You are using fantasy to escape reality" Shinji "Why can't I dream that I'm alone" Rei "That is not a dream that is a substitute for reality" Shinji "So where is my dream?" Rei "it is a continuation of reality" Shinji "But where is my reality" Rei "It is at the end of your dream"

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Dec 05, 2013 3:25 am Shinji "Hey" Rei "What is it?" Shinji "What are dreams?" Rei "Dreams?....Yes, dreams"

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Thu Dec 05, 2013 3:35 am NemZ wrote: Oh, and now we're back to asserting that reality is just the subjective dream of 'truth' that we give credence to. Great.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Dec 05, 2013 5:07 am Well, maybe Rei was subtly trying to convince Shinji that Instrumentality is really not what he wants? Personally I interpreted that differently : Shinji "Hey" Rei "What is it?" Shinji "What are dreams?" Rei "Dreams?....Yes, dreams"

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Thu Dec 05, 2013 5:33 am ElMariachi wrote: You can see all the interactions between Shinji and Rei prior to his rejection of instrumentality as Rei making Shinji get a hold of himself : the past events destroyed him, so he only wants to be left alone to stop suffering, and thinks that Instrumentality can give him that, so Rei shows him that Instrumentality can't give him that, and that he can't have that no matter what, so he should stop thinking about it and concentrate on objectively judging Instrumentality for what it is. And once Shinji get over his wish to be left alone and start seeing it objectively, he rejects it because it sucks and is not a path mankind should take.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Dec 05, 2013 12:58 pm I don't believe so much that Rei is trying to convince Shinji to reject instrumentality, but rather that after a complete and logical statement of the facts of instrumentality, Shinji chooses to leave after having experienced the mental q-tip cleaning from the crazy Asuka journey. Shinji's changed enough to see that Instrumentality isn't something he wants, which is why he responds the way that he does to Rei.

The psychological dream scenes in the Pre 3I Instrumentality are seemingly having four parts: - the sandbox
